Operation 'batchCreateEvents' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, poor_ai_interpretability. The original summary and description are too generic and lack critical details for AI agent decision-making. The improved version clarifies: (1) the batch nature and efficiency benefit, (2) the expected input structure (array of events), (3) the domain context (digital experience analytics), (4) the side effect (events are stored for analytics), and (5) the return confirmation. This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation versus single event creation and what to expect from the response.

data.current_description: Batch import multiple events in a single request.
data.summary_suggestion: Create multiple events in a single batch request
data.description_suggestion: Creates multiple events for digital experience analytics in a single batch request. Accepts a BatchCreateEventsRequest containing an array of events. Each event is processed and stored for analytics tracking. Returns a response confirming successful creation of events. This operation is more efficient than creating events individually when importing multiple events simultaneously.
data.reasoning: The original summary and description are too generic and lack critical details for AI agent decision-making. The improved version clarifies: (1) the batch nature and efficiency benefit, (2) the expected input structure (array of events), (3) the domain context (digital experience analytics), (4) the side effect (events are stored for analytics), and (5) the return confirmation. This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation versus single event creation and what to expect from the response.

data.current_description: Retrieve a list of users matching the supplied filter criteria.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieve a paginated list of users filtered by the supplied criteria. Accepts filter parameters to narrow results. Returns an array of user objects with pagination metadata. Use this operation to search for specific users or retrieve user lists for bulk operations.
data.reasoning: Current description is too vague about what filters are available and what the response contains. Added details about pagination, return format, and use cases to help AI agents understand when and how to use this operation effectively.

message: Operation 'deleteUserByUid' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_context, missing_side_effects. Summary needed clarification that UID is an external identifier. Description lacked critical information about permanence, scope of deletion (including associated data), and irreversibility. Added context to distinguish this from deleteUserById and help AI agents choose the correct operation.

data.current_description: Delete a user record identified by the uid query parameter.
data.summary_suggestion: Delete user by external UID identifier
data.description_suggestion: Permanently delete a user record identified by the external uid query parameter. This operation removes all associated user data and activity records. The deletion is irreversible. Use this when removing users identified by their external identifier rather than FullStory ID.
data.reasoning: Summary needed clarification that UID is an external identifier. Description lacked critical information about permanence, scope of deletion (including associated data), and irreversibility. Added context to distinguish this from deleteUserById and help AI agents choose the correct operation.

data.current_summary: Delete a user by FullStory ID
data.current_description: Delete a user record identified by the FullStory-generated ID.
data.description_suggestion: Permanently delete a user record identified by their FullStory-generated ID. This operation removes all associated user data and activity records. The deletion is irreversible. Use this when removing users identified by their FullStory ID rather than external uid.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate, but description lacks critical information about permanence, scope of deletion, and irreversibility. Added context to clarify this is a destructive operation and to distinguish it from deleteUserByUid for better AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'batchCreateOrUpdateUsers' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Current description is too minimal. Added details about input format (array), operation type (upsert per user), response structure, the important side effect about analytics visibility, and use case context to help AI agents understand batch operation semantics and when to use it.

data.current_description: Create or update multiple users in a single request.
data.description_suggestion: Create or update multiple users in a single batch request. Accepts an array of user objects and performs upsert operations on each. Returns an array of created/updated user responses with status for each user. Like individual upsert operations, users will not appear in search/analytics until activity is associated. Useful for bulk user synchronization.
data.reasoning: Current description is too minimal. Added details about input format (array), operation type (upsert per user), response structure, the important side effect about analytics visibility, and use case context to help AI agents understand batch operation semantics and when to use it.

message: Operation 'createEvent' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Summary is adequate. Description needed clarification about the mutually-exclusive association requirement, return value details, immutability, and when events appear in analytics. data.current_description: Creates one event with the specified details. Events can be associated with a user by uid or session by session_id.
data.description_suggestion: Create a single event with the specified details. Events must be associated with either a user (via uid) or a session (via session_id). Returns the created event object with FullStory-generated event ID and timestamp. Events are immutable after creation and appear in analytics immediately upon successful creation.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate. Description needed clarification about the mutually-exclusive association requirement, return value details, immutability, and when events appear in analytics. These details help AI agents understand event creation semantics and constraints.
